residual_risks: The new dark indigo body text (#1e1b4b) and translucent white Nav (bg-white/50) are layered over a pastel gradient (body::before). Text/border contrast against the lightest gradient regions (near #cffafe cyan) was not verified against WCAG thresholds; this is a visual/accessibility concern rather than a logic bug, but could affect readability in bright areas of the gradient.

tailwind.config.js adds an unused `ink: '#1e1b4b'` color token that is not referenced anywhere in the diff (globals.css hardcodes the hex value directly instead of using the token). This is inert/dead configuration, not a functional bug.

pages/garden/[slug].js keeps `border-gray-200` on the References section divider, which is visually inconsistent with the new indigo-themed borders used elsewhere (Card, Nav). Purely cosmetic, not a logic error.
testing_gaps: Repo has no test infrastructure (package.json 'test' script is just 'next build'); verified via `npm run build` that the change compiles and all 54 static pages (including all /blog/[slug] and /garden/[slug] routes) generate successfully, but there is no automated visual/regression check for the new gradient background, font application, or color changes across breakpoints.
No verification was performed of `next/font/google` (Space Grotesk) network fetch behavior in restricted/offline build environments beyond the successful local build in this sandbox.
